normalized_rule: Project authority outranks SAGE-Kit defaults; SAGE-Kit authority outranks optional adapter and agent defaults without overriding host system authority.
notes: Proposed ownership only; preserve project-specific authority and approval gates.

normalized_rule: Non-trivial work requires an adopted active SPEC, phase, task, normalized manifest, execution packet, or explicit equivalent; path location alone is not authority.
notes: Templates retain the authority-reference field.

normalized_rule: Governance is selected per control scope: start Light and upgrade to Standard or Heavy only on defined risk triggers; parent Heavy governance does not make every worker Heavy.
notes: Preserve Light, Standard, and Heavy user outcomes.

normalized_rule: Governance level and permission mode are independent; every active controller or worker records both and receives only the named mutation surface.
notes: Retain packet fields; replace duplicated definitions with pointers.

normalized_rule: Project Manager decision, Coder implementation/orchestration, independent Final Review verdict, and acceptance remain separate authorities.
notes: Controller topology remains in Session Orchestration; authority meaning is proposed here.

normalized_rule: Final Review remains read-only; correction uses separately CORRECTIVE_AUTHORIZED workers and review verdict ownership stays independent from correction.
notes: Engineering System wording can imply that the reviewer itself may receive corrective write authority.

normalized_rule: Commit, push, merge, publish, release, and cleanup require a distinct post-verdict SUBMIT_AUTHORIZED grant; implementation or review authority does not imply submit authority.
notes: A separate node is optional; the permission boundary is not.

normalized_rule: Finding ownership does not grant waiver authority; waivers require the named Waiver Authority or explicit delegation and human-only gates remain with their named human authority.
notes: Generic project-owner waiver wording must not broaden authority.

normalized_rule: Approval is explicit and target-scoped; agents cannot infer, broaden, or cross closed approval gates or perform runtime, destructive, external, or submit mutations beyond the grant.
notes: Project instances continue to own their exact gates.

normalized_rule: A launch-only delta may clarify reporting or order but cannot change scope, gates, permissions, ownership, contracts, or runtime authority; missing or contradictory authority fails closed before edits.
notes: Templates retain envelope fields and a pointer.

normalized_rule: Configured active-context and routing files have one serial owner; parallel workers return proposals and only an owner with matching permission applies updates.
notes: Location map: SPEC Source=canonical-context; Harness=proposed operational owner; Core and Skill=reference; both hooks=retain platform enforcement.

normalized_rule: Resolve one current execution source in the order explicit source, configured mapping, ACTIVE_CONTEXT pointer, then explicitly enabled legacy adapter; explicit or configured failure has no legacy fallback.
notes: Location map: SPEC Source=canonical; Core/Harness/Skill/generic references=convert normative copy to pointer while retaining routing guidance.

normalized_rule: ACTIVE_SPEC is executable authority; ACTIVE_CONTEXT is a compact handoff; ACCEPTED_HISTORY and REFERENCE_ONLY are non-executable; transient run state belongs under runtime state.
notes: Do not promote history or handoff summaries into execution authority.

normalized_rule: Default adoption is package-bound and version or digest bound; installed Skill and generic templates are not project authority; legacy projects remain supported without forced migration.
notes: Vendoring remains explicit legacy compatibility.

normalized_rule: ACTIVE_CONTEXT is current handoff truth, not history or runtime state; it stays compact and links to authority rather than copying it.
notes: Harness may retain only its serial-write delta.

normalized_rule: Load active authority and capability metadata first, then only task-relevant Skill or reference bodies; broaden context only for a named correctness, safety, or acceptance decision.
notes: Reduces repeated startup context.

normalized_rule: Accepted history keeps its selected contract and is not batch rewritten; current work uses current checks while immutable accepted work receives historical integrity checks.
notes: Core retains the general principle; this path owns selector semantics.

normalized_rule: Validation scope authority is invocation manifest, project manifest, structured active context, then conservative current-or-ambiguous handling; closeout alone cannot authorize immutable history.
notes: No duplicate authority established.

normalized_rule: Begin with one bounded loop; admit a graph only for a concrete dependency, independent context or tool boundary, safe parallel work and join, independent evaluator, authority separation, or state boundary.
notes: Graph is never mandatory for Light work.

normalized_rule: Before choosing execution shape record the dependency DAG, parallel candidates, serial barriers, and phase-internal lanes; SERIAL needs a concrete dependency, ownership conflict, gate, or runtime constraint.
notes: Planning retains activation; packet retains fields.

normalized_rule: Parallel writable lanes require mutually exclusive writable ownership; shared files have one serial integration owner and overlapping lanes must merge, reassign, or become read-only before edits.
Public contract freeze, shared-file integration, approval gates, final integration, context or ledger updates, closeout, and real shared runtime smoke stay serial unless ownership is exclusive.
notes: Do not flatten stronger runtime or isolation gates.

normalized_rule: A failed readiness item serializes only the affected node; unrelated candidates remain eligible, and milestone-wide SERIAL is used only when the barrier cannot be isolated.
notes: Prevents broad serialization without decision value.

normalized_rule: Do not repartition active execution by default; apply a new graph or wave shape at the next safe barrier unless active authority permits repartitioning.
notes: No runtime Graph evolution is authorized in Stage 1.

normalized_rule: The usual wave pattern is serial setup, parallel read-only discovery, serial contract and ownership freeze, parallel disjoint writes, parallel isolated validation, then serial integration and handoff.
notes: This is a guide pattern, not mandatory topology.

normalized_rule: Heavy milestone flow is PM issue, Coder orchestration and result, PM structural gate, independent Final Review, bounded correction or re-review, PM decision, then ledger and closeout.
notes: Entry Gate currently omits part of this chain.

normalized_rule: Coder normally orchestrates workers; direct edits are limited to narrow named controller-owned integration or packet work with explicit ownership, tests, stops, and independent review.
notes: A narrow integration worker may collapse into Coder only under this rule.

normalized_rule: Use normally one Coder Controller and one Final Review Controller; add controllers only for separate scope, context contamination, specialized review, or independent runtime ownership.
notes: Primary anti-duplication and collapsibility guard.

normalized_rule: Lane results distinguish DONE, DONE_WITH_CONCERNS, HANDOFF, and BLOCKED; HANDOFF is nonterminal and DONE_WITH_CONCERNS cannot auto-advance.
notes: Wave Execution omits HANDOFF from one status list while templates permit it.

normalized_rule: Worktree isolation is optional and PM-authorized; PM names mode, count, base, naming, eligible scopes, serial files, runtime, integration, submit, and cleanup ownership.
notes: Final Review cannot create or mutate its review worktree.

normalized_rule: Worktrees enable parallelism only with disjoint writes and nonconflicting runtime or dependency ownership; cleanup requires preservation, evidence, process checks, and a separately authorized owner.
notes: Never infer cleanup authority from implementation or review.

normalized_rule: C0 record-only work receives targeted consistency verification; C1 may auto-correct only in a bounded authorized envelope; C2 needs matching semantic authority; C3 external or destructive work needs human decision.
notes: Template retains change-class and authority fields.

normalized_rule: Each unit uses one primary review topology; Light has focused verification by default, Standard adds affected-lane review when required, and Heavy has lane or wave review plus one final integration review; reviewer stacking needs concrete risk.
notes: Do not make an independent reviewer mandatory for Light.

normalized_rule: Verification escalates focused, affected-lane, then final integration; expensive verification is eligible only for a matching frozen candidate after review and corrective closure and runs once per candidate and kind.
notes: Current tests phrase-couple the duplicate Skill text.

normalized_rule: Final verification attempt state is PREFLIGHT, READY, STARTED, then PASSED, FAILED, or ABORTED; only STARTED consumes budget and attempt identity is idempotent across resume.
notes: Continuity owns persisted representation, not the decision.

normalized_rule: A failed verification node skips only dependent successors while independent nodes continue; the helper is not a general scheduler.
notes: Do not expand this into a Stage 1 scheduler.

normalized_rule: A frozen candidate binds repository inputs, review closure, corrective closure, contracts, and dependencies; clean-head is default, working-tree mode is explicit and digest-bound, and modes never silently fall back.
notes: Preserve candidate fingerprint version compatibility.

normalized_rule: Evidence is immutable at its original identity; reuse is a separate decision, unchanged matching evidence is reused, and invalidation is limited to affected paths, contracts, dependencies, toolchains, platforms, authority versions, or candidate-bound evidence.
notes: Runtime implementation is not a competing documentation authority.

normalized_rule: Finding-count or severity reduction resets no-progress; one stagnant same-root round is recorded, two consecutive stagnant rounds are BLOCKED, and a local event limit produces HANDOFF_READY rather than a false blocker.
notes: Location map: Economy=proposed canonical; convergence/review modules=implementation evidence; Session, Corrective and Structural Gate conflicting copies=pointer/correction.

normalized_rule: A Preauthorized Convergence Window is explicit and opt-in, binds scope, paths, invariant, root-cause family, approval and stops, permits only implementation-preserving successors, and hands off on authority or scope expansion.
notes: Do not make the window automatic.

normalized_rule: Initial review batches findings once; corrective re-review checks original closure, direct regressions, new P0/P1, and authority or false-green blocking P2; unrelated ordinary P2/P3 enters backlog.
notes: A stronger runtime safety category remains an unresolved narrow reconciliation.

normalized_rule: Deterministic Closure is allowed only for a pre-authored MECHANICAL_STATUS predicate binding finding, fields, authority, allowed diff, checks, hashes, receipt owner, and verdict; ambiguity falls back to targeted or full review.
notes: Do not move this subsection in the minimal Stage 1 diff.

normalized_rule: Ordinary EOF, final-newline, or trailing-whitespace findings in exact writable scope route to byte-bound normalization and focused checks, never to a product blocker; protected or semantic bytes fail closed.
notes: Historical and checksum-bound content is excluded.

normalized_rule: Completion requires fresh scope-relevant evidence and no unresolved blocking gate; runtime or UI evidence is required only when that surface is in scope, with statuses PASS, FAIL, BLOCKED, WAIVED, or N/A.
notes: Proposed owner nomination; exact gate catalog remains project-owned.

normalized_rule: A no-action result is valid only when it names inspected scope, the applicable decision, and still-valid evidence; it must be contract-permitted and never silently converted to PASS.
notes: Not current authority or runtime capability; proposed owner is unaccepted.

normalized_rule: Project Owner Entry turns a non-technical idea into planning inputs through five outcome questions and follow-ups only when needed; it does not authorize implementation or directly create an executable roadmap.
notes: Preserve the low-friction idea entry path.

normalized_rule: The Project Profile owns durable product summary, users, problems, goals, non-goals, constraints, requirements, acceptance signals, privacy boundaries, runtime/data ownership, and project vocabulary.
notes: An adopted instance may become project authority; the source template does not.

normalized_rule: Technical Design owns architecture, components, data ownership, public contracts, runtime boundaries, error handling, security, testing strategy, and architectural non-goals.
notes: Preserve architecture outcome coverage.

normalized_rule: A broad idea is mapped to user, operator, data, integration, runtime, observability, security, and support capabilities; only in-scope capabilities are retained and one primary capability anchors each milestone candidate.
notes: Suggested categories are prompts, not invented requirements.

normalized_rule: A milestone candidate becomes executable only after granularity review shows one primary capability, observable result, bounded ownership, reviewability, and named verification; broad candidates remain planning material and are split.
notes: Do not replace planning depth with arbitrary file or count ceilings.

normalized_rule: The roadmap sequences design, foundation, product, hardening, and release outcomes; each milestone has one capability, inputs, deliverables, validation, closeout, non-goals, and required phase decomposition.
notes: ROADMAP_TEMPLATE is an explicit compatibility alias.

normalized_rule: Implementation begins only after the first milestone has an accepted entry gate and phase documents name objective, non-goals, file and ownership boundaries, contracts, verification, runtime smoke, optional profiles, and stop conditions.
notes: Preserve entry and phase user outcomes.

normalized_rule: The Milestone Execution Packet carries readable authority input from PM to Coder: source refs, governance, permissions, DAG, ownership, worktree, evidence, candidate, adapter, gate, stop, and handoff fields.
notes: Retain fields while moving generic normative prose to owners.

normalized_rule: The Milestone Result Packet reports implemented and unimplemented scope, governance, permissions, evidence, skips, gaps, and stop conditions from Coder to PM; DONE is not acceptance and HANDOFF is nonterminal.
notes: PM performs structural gating before Final Review.

normalized_rule: Final Review produces an independent verdict, correction uses bounded workers and closure verification, and only PM or project owner records acceptance after an acceptable verdict.
notes: Submission records delivery and is not acceptance.

normalized_rule: Closeout order is Final Review verdict, corrective closure or explicit stop, PM or owner decision, ledger update, then compact historical closeout; HANDOFF cannot close and closeout is not startup context.
notes: Entry Gate lists ledger and closeout without the full review and PM decision chain.

normalized_rule: The SAGE-Kit Skill activates only for explicit SAGE-Kit scope or adoption; generic planning, review, or debugging does not trigger it.
notes: Do not broaden the trigger while thinning the Skill.

normalized_rule: The Skill detects activation, resolves project authority, selects governance, and routes only to task-relevant canonical owners and host references; it is not a second governance framework.
notes: Retain minimal fail-closed and host-routing invariants.

normalized_rule: Optional capabilities follow Detect, Authorize, Bound, Invoke, Capture, Map, and Fallback and default to metadata-only or read-only behavior.
notes: Guides may retain task-shaped checklists and examples.

normalized_rule: Missing optional capability is not product failure when a safe native path exists; BLOCKED applies only when active authority makes it required and no safe fallback exists.
notes: Dedicated end-to-end fallback coverage is a scenario candidate.

normalized_rule: Adapter authorization is separate from SAGE-Kit role permission; both are required and environment, destructive, or submit writes remain separately gated.
notes: Location map: Capability Adapters=canonical; execution reference=pointer/checklist; host references=retain platform mapping only.

normalized_rule: Do not silently install adapters or change global or environment configuration; exact writes, current provider guidance, rollback, fallback, and explicit approval are prerequisites.
notes: Stage 0 installs nothing.

normalized_rule: Runtime-specific adapter bans and overrides outrank generic availability, propagate through delegation, and degrade to equivalent native behavior without becoming a false product blocker.
notes: Location map: Capability Adapters=canonical; Skill/execution=pointer; openai metadata and launch templates=retain only minimal pre-load enforcement.

normalized_rule: Host enforcement is labeled hard, managed or best-effort, or soft; unknown invocation, permission, delegation, persistence, containment, and resume behavior is never claimed as enforced.
notes: Preserve cross-platform capability honesty.

normalized_rule: File, directory, record, template, or capability prose does not activate an optional profile; activation requires explicit project-owned authority.
notes: Universal owner is a proposal; state-machine and control-plane intent is inferred, not a current defect.

normalized_rule: Task Dispatch activation criteria guide a PM decision and do not automatically trigger the profile; Light work does not inherit it unless explicitly enabled.
notes: Preserve ordinary phase-document path for small work.

normalized_rule: Task Dispatch contract selection happens before validation: explicit v2 selects current v2; v0 or v1 require terminal records and trusted immutable-container authority; mixed, active-unversioned, unsupported, or conflicting inputs fail closed.
notes: Runtime modules remain executable contract implementations.

normalized_rule: After a contract version is selected, failure remains a failure of that version and never triggers validation under another version.
notes: Location map: compatibility doc=canonical selector; profile docs=references; version modules=implementation evidence. False-green critical rule.

normalized_rule: A Validation Scope Manifest is exact migration authority for target-relative containers and frozen v0 or v1 history; it rejects globs, ranges, traversal, aliases, duplicates, nonterminal work, and merging invocation and project manifests.
notes: It is not routine runtime state.

normalized_rule: Frozen v0 and v1 are distinct semantic snapshots while v2 remains bound to the current validator; current and historical contracts are not flattened.
notes: Location map: compatibility doc=human selection owner; validator modules/bundles=executable version owners; v0/v1 resources remain pinned and v2 remains current.

normalized_rule: Frozen policy, schema, rules, validator sidecars, and engine are digest and provenance bound; corruption, missing artifact, provenance mismatch, or engine mismatch is a validation failure.
notes: Location map: frozen validator=executable owner; each listed v0/v1 file=immutable compatibility resource; documentation=human contract. No Stage 1 change.

normalized_rule: Active or new Task Dispatch records carry matching v2 metadata and canonical policy identity; task and evidence select the same version and digest.
notes: Location map: compatibility doc=selection; profile=application; source schemas=machine shape; packaged schemas/policy=canonical-JSON compatibility resources.

normalized_rule: Closed legacy history is validated pair-by-pair and only then excluded from active ID, lease, lock, and dispatch-board reconciliation; active, current, and ambiguous records remain strict.
notes: Closeout alone is not immutable-history authority.

normalized_rule: When Task Dispatch is active, acceptance requires matching task and evidence pairs, complete board membership or archival authority, no orphan records, no overlapping active exclusive locks, and state-truth reconciliation.
notes: Applies only when the profile is active.

normalized_rule: Validator success establishes structured-record completeness, not product correctness; Final Review still inspects actual evidence and changed surfaces.
notes: Prevents validator false green.

normalized_rule: An execution-document project lock selects one exact contract version without fallback; selection semantics do not belong to either selected version.
notes: Compatibility document owns cross-version selection; each version contract owns only its local identity.

normalized_rule: Source execution contracts, runtime resources, and packaged documentation mirrors retain their defined relationship: execution-document files are byte-identical; v2 Task Dispatch schemas are canonical-JSON equivalent; frozen v0 and v1 are pinned semantic snapshots.
notes: Representative exact roots are listed; report section 3 defines and validates the complete version-directory families. Packaged resources are not independent business authority.

normalized_rule: The project selects an exact resource contract and each phase selects the bound profile or records N/A with reason; missing or unknown selections fail closed.
notes: Location map: resource contract=policy owner; execution-document contract/schema/profile files=selection fields and bindings; packaged counterparts remain byte mirrors.

normalized_rule: When adopted, state-machine transitions have stable IDs, mutations emit event or audit records, replay explains durable state, visible transitions have assertions, and real external effects retain approval gates.
notes: Presence alone is not proven activation authority.

normalized_rule: When adopted, the control plane owns product state, users, permissions, audit, UI, browser APIs, and orchestration while the execution boundary owns scoped execution and local observation; bypass, agent-owned authorization, hidden mutation, secret exposure, and unknown-error success are forbidden.
notes: BOUNDARY_TEMPLATE is a declared compatibility alias and must not be deleted casually.

normalized_rule: The Structural Gate checks Coder result-packet completeness before independent review and records post-review corrective or closure routing without performing technical review or PM acceptance.
notes: Retain capability, scope, governance, permission, ownership, evidence, tests, runtime smoke, gate, skip, blocker, receipt, waiver, convergence and PM-pending fields.

normalized_rule: Execution-document version 2026.7.19.3 owns its frozen local schemas, profiles and digest identity independently of current versions and package release.
notes: Historical contract owns only its version-local identity; packaged copies remain byte mirrors.

normalized_rule: Execution-document version 2026.7.20.1 owns its local schemas, profiles and digest identity independently of historical versions and package release.
notes: Current contract owns only its version-local identity; packaged copies remain byte mirrors.
